data.title: Acerinox, S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• Acerinox, S.A. press release (OTCPK:ANIOY): FY Profit for the year amounted to €225M.
  • EBITDA, €500M, was 29% lower than that of 2023, with a sales margin of 9%
  • The Group’s revenue during the fiscal year amounted to €5,413M (18% lower than that of 2023).

data.title: bpost NV/SA reports Q4 results
data.description:
• bpost NV/SA press release (OTCPK:BPOSF): Q4 Group operating income at €1,335M, +9.7% or +€117.8M compared to last year, including 214.1 mEUR contribution from Staci which has been consolidated as of August 1, 2024.
  • Group adjusted EBIT at €84M (margin of 6.3%) versus last year at 74.1 mEUR.
  • Group reported EBIT at -€222.9M, down by -€293.8M compared to last year.

data.title: Proximus PLC reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Proximus PLC press release (OTCPK:BGAOF): Q4 Net income of €80M
  • Revenue of €1.69B (+10.5% Y/Y).

data.title: Vidrala reports FY results
data.description:
• Vidrala, S.A. press release (OTCPK:VDRFF): FY GAAP EPS of €8.85.
  • Revenue of €1.59B (+1.9% Y/Y).
  • Operating profit, EBITDA, amounted to EUR 454.0 million representing an operating margin of 28.6%.
  • Net debt as of December 31, 2024 was reduced to 248.3 million, equivalent to 0.6x times last twelve months EBITDA.

data.title: Bitcoin falls below $80,000, at risk of erasing all post-election gains
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) dropped below $80,000 in the early hours of Friday, losing more than 25% since its record high on January 20 - President Trump's inauguration - in a dramatic price correction, as looming tariffs drive safe-haven demand.

The top cryptocurrency had been one of the most popular Trump trades, given expectations of a friendlier regulatory environment under the new administration. But risk appetite has been on the decline since Trump ramped up tariff threats for Canada, Mexico, China, and even the European Union.

The crypto selloff deepened on Friday: bitcoin (BTC-USD) -6.6%, ether (ETH-USD) -9.1%, XRP (XRP-USD) -7.6%, Solana (SOL-USD) -5.7% at the time of writing.

If the risk-off sentiment continues, bitcoin (BTC-USD) could lose all its gains from the post-election rally and outflows from crypto ETFs could swell further. The crypto fear and greed index, which tracks trader sentiment in the sector, is now flashing "Extreme Fear."

"Instead of serving as an alternative to fiat instability, as its proponents have long claimed, crypto has once again been treated like other high-risk assets and sold off accordingly," SA analyst TMC Research noted in a recent analysis.

Still, crypto bulls are hopeful of a rebound in bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices. Geoffrey Kendrick, head of digital assets research at Standard Chartered, told CNBC on Thursday that he still expects the top crypto to reach $200,000 this year as the industry sees more institutionalization and regulatory clarity.

Crypto-related stocks to watch: Coinbase (COIN), Strategy (MSTR), Riot Platforms (RIOT), Hut 8 (HUT), MARA (MARA), CleanSpark (CLSK), Cipher Mining (CIFR), Hive Digital (HIVE), Stronghold Digital (SDIG), Bakkt (BKKT), Bitdeer (BTDR), Bitfarm (BITF), Bit Digital (BTBT).

data.title: Google plans job cuts at HR, cloud units amid cost reduction moves - report
data.description:
Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Google informed employees in its “People Operations” and cloud organizations this week that it plans to cut jobs as part of internal reorganizations, CNBC reported.

The tech giant will offer a voluntary exit program to U.S.-based, full-time employees in its human relations unit known as People Operations, beginning in early March, the report added, citing a memo issued by HR chief Fiona Cicconi.

Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Last month, it was reported that the company had offered the voluntary exit program to employees working in the Platforms & Devices group.

The move comes on the heels of Alphabet CFO Anat Ashkenazi noting that one of her top priorities would be to carry out more cost-cutting as Google expands its spending on AI infrastructure in 2025, the report added.

Under the exit program for HR employees, those who are level 4 and level 5 may receive a severance of 14 weeks of salary and one more week for every full year of service, according to the memo.

Separately, the company has laid off employees in several teams within its cloud unit, mainly impacting operations support staff. Some of the moves include moving roles to other countries, the report added, citing sources and different internal memos.

On Wednesday, Bloomberg News had reported the job cuts at Google’s cloud division.

“Our teams have continued to make changes to operate more efficiently, remove layers, and ensure they are set up for long term success,” said Google spokesperson Brandon Asberry to the media outlet in a statement. “This work is ongoing as we continue to invest in our company’s biggest priorities and the significant opportunities ahead.”

The job cuts at the company’s cloud unit impacted the division's sales operations, customer experience, internal deal and go-to-market teams. Some affected employees’ roles are being relocated to India and Mexico City, the report added.

The company confirmed to CNBC that the changes include consolidating or opening roles in other places in the U.S. and abroad. The largest employee presence for the cloud unit is still in the U.S., and this has not changed.

The number of job cuts was unclear, but the company noted that it is small in quantity and that it continues to hire for critical sales and engineering roles, the report added.

data.title: Fugro reports FY results
data.description:
• Fugro press release (OTCPK:FUGRF): FY GAAP EPS of €2.44.
  • Revenue of €2.28B (+4.1% Y/Y).

data.title: Alpha Bank reports FY results
data.description:
• Alpha Bank press release (OTCPK:ALBKY): FY Core PPI broadly flat at €1.3bn (-0.4% Y/Y)
  • NII reached €1.6bn, down -0.7% Y/Y.
  • Normalised Profit After Tax of €196mn in Q4.

data.title: National Bank of Greece reports FY results
data.description:
• National Bank of Greece press release (OTCPK:NBGIF): FY \Group Core PAT1 at €1.3b (+10% yoy), with better-than-expected top line resilience.
  • Recurring OpEx up +5%2 yoy in FY24 reflecting class leading investments in IT and increasing wages. C:CI ratio at 32%, well inside our FY24 target of less than 33%.
  • Core RoTE1 at 17.5% in FY24 before adjusting for excess capital

data.title: TikTok to park $8.8B for data centers in Thailand - report
